pairs.label: 24 (image, caption) pairs
pairs.detail: Piece renders captioned with the trigger word wtrclrchess; drawings from the room join the same shape when persisted.

adapter.label: wtrclrchess LoRA (illustrative)
adapter.status: No image adapter was trained for this workshop. This card shows the provenance a real one carries: base model, dataset hash, config hash, runner.

evaluation.label: 12 held-out captions
evaluation.status: Cached illustrative values below; a real run needs a vision judge.

before.label: Base output (illustrative)
before.caption: the prompt without the trigger word: a clean, unstylized bishop

after.label: Adapted output (illustrative)
after.caption: a wtrclrchess style white bishop, soft watercolor edges, plain background

payload.metrics_note: Illustrative cached values. The trade-off is the honest part: pushing style hard usually costs a little identity.
payload.media_provenance: Both images composed locally by tools/make_media.py from the CC BY-SA 3.0 Cburnett bishop; no diffusion model produced them.
payload.note: The same conceptual chain the text page runs for real: pairs in, adapter out, eval always. Here every element is a reviewed cached illustration, because live image training is impractical in the room; the card says which parts are real files and which are illustrative numbers.
